name: uPAR CAR T cells cleared senescent cells in pathology models
summary: The foundational uPAR CAR T study supports immune-engineered senolysis in vivo, but only in preclinical pathology models.
statement: uPAR-specific CAR T cells eliminated senescent cells in vitro and in vivo, extended survival in a mouse lung adenocarcinoma model treated with senescence-inducing therapy, and restored tissue homeostasis in mouse liver fibrosis models.
population_or_model: Senescent cell systems plus mouse lung cancer and liver fibrosis models
quantitative_note: The source reports in vivo senescent-cell ablation, extended survival in treated lung adenocarcinoma-bearing mice, and liver tissue-homeostasis restoration after uPAR CAR T treatment.
caveats: The strongest in vivo results are disease and injury models, not natural human aging.
uPAR is not exclusive to senescent cells, so target-specificity and tissue-context safety remain central.
The source discloses patent interests related to the CAR T approach.
